notes: The card-effect correctness release: a full second-pass sweep of the effect engine fixed ~150 card bugs where a card looked like it resolved but quietly did the wrong thing — buffs that never applied, conditions that were ignored, activation costs that were skipped (paid for free), immunities that lasted forever, 'you may' downsides you could skip for free, and abilities that were silently dead. Highlights: Sentomaru no longer instantly loses you the game on any 'search your deck' card; Dosun (OP06-030) can no longer become permanently unkillable in battle; negated Characters correctly lose [Rush]/[Double Attack]/[Banish]; 'if this would be K.O.'d, pay X instead' protections (Morley etc.) now fire on battle K.O.s and bounces; dual-type '{A} or {B}' effects no longer ignore the second type; conditional counters (OP08-096, Gum-Gum Giant) now honor their conditions; mandatory 'trash from hand' / self-K.O. downsides can no longer be skipped for free; and a long list of dead reactive and base-power abilities now work. See the full grouped changelog on the release page. Board & UI: player names show everywhere instead of 'South/North'; opponent status tags read right-side-up; the card preview drops a redundant name; the 'Show Result' button no longer sits on the opponent's hand; 'Ready — waiting for opponent' is readable; black decks look black; and the updater's 'What's New' now shows what actually changed. Coming from v1.0.17? This also bundles everything from v1.0.18: an AI overhaul with a smooth Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced difficulty curve (all three share one strong brain, lower tiers looser on purpose; the AI now pressures your life, stops over-spending counters, holds big counters back, commits DON one attacker at a time, and mulligans with more nuance), more '…, and …' two-part card fixes (Punk Vise, Lim, White Snake, Gum-Gum Giant Sumo Slap), and a friends blocked-list with a two-step block confirm.
